findings.claim: Q4 is labelled ELF-TYPE-002 (inference, 'one logical inch beyond the text'), but the keyed option A is a near-verbatim restatement of the passage rather than an inference.
findings.evidence: Passage: 'a firm that bought those cabinets found the folding sheet was the sheet that fitted the drawer.' Option A (key): 'a firm buying cabinets found the folding sheet was the one that fitted its drawers.'
findings.why: The cognitive demand of the key is closer to retrieval than inference, so the 'inference' family label is generous. It does NOT overturn the SOUND verdict: distractor B is also a passage echo (the office's OWN filing decision), so the item still discriminates on the intended axis (spread-mechanism vs own-choice) driven by the stem 'spread ... among ordinary firms', and no surface-word-match tell reliably scores the key. Real-but-minor label observation only.
